reason: The statement applies because the text fragment describes Nehemiah, a Hebrew leader, who commands Hebrew soldiers in his role as governor of Judah. Nehemiah is shown rebuking the nobles and rulers and setting an assembly against them, as well as calling for the restoration of lands and properties to his fellow Hebrews. He also mentions his brethren and servants, indicating his authority over Hebrew soldiers. Therefore, the statement accurately describes the content of the text fragment.
